title: C++ code giải bài tập
tail:
024 maps, flat face normals and perspective-correct depth, foreground-only SSIM. A test is valid only if average normal/depth SSIM >=0.9. Score rewards using as few simplified vertices as possible.

Current status: our best Kattis score is only 81.938904 on 7/7. Public top/top-1 target is about 91+, and the required target for this project is 91.80+. The old local/history approaches plateau around 81.93: macro selectors, QEM/edge-collapse variants, star/grid/sphere/torus recognizers, hidden carrier attempts, and small parameter sweeps are not enough. A 0.3 improvement after many hours is not acceptable; we need a decisive gap-closing breakthrough, not a tiny tweak.

Your job is not to brainstorm only. Implement a complete ready-to-submit C++17 solution that plausibly jumps toward 91.80+. Think broadly and deeply: infer hidden case families from the repo and submissions, exploit vertex-only Hausdorff safely, use constructive remeshing/recognizers/selectors, create new strategy families, or rewrite the solver. If your idea resembles the 81.93 plateau, either combine it with a genuinely new path or abandon it.

Deliverable requirements:
1. Return an actual downloadable file named submission_next.cpp if the UI supports it.
2. Also include the full standalone C++17 code block as fallback.
3. It must compile with: g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -pipe submission_next.cpp -o solution
4. It must read stdin and write stdout only.
5. It must pass the official sample with first output line exactly: 8 12
6. Do not stop with explanation, pseudo-code, or âsubmit this laterâ. Produce complete code.
7. Spend serious long reasoning time. Do not conclude early unless you have a complete C++ implementation ready.

Treat this as a must-hit 91.80+ attempt. The central manager will compile, sample-test, submit every candidate, and feed back exact Kattis results.
